entry_condition: P4-G3 v2 == WEAK_GO -> full 3-scorer x 7-arm x 10-seed matrix

H1_pc_cng_intrinsically_valuable.reason: A6 (rule_pc_cng) candidates are byte-identical to A2 (random_corruption) in all 500 groups of the frozen v1 manifest; the v1 benchmark carries no rule PC-CNG signal.

H2_chemformer_simply_stronger.reason: Chemformer-LoRA has the WEAKEST positive-only baseline (A0 MRR 0.376922) yet shows the largest augmentation gains; scorer strength alone cannot explain the augmentation pattern.

H3_pc_cng_candidates_harder.reason: A6 ≡ A2, so PC-CNG hardness is undefined on v1. Descriptively, the best-gaining arms differ per scorer and do not coincide with the hardest source, so difficulty alone does not explain the gains.

smiles_validity.reason: Sources with valid_fraction < 0.5 are majority-unparseable SMILES. Structure-based scorers (GNN, Morgan MLP) silently drop these candidates, degrading those arms toward the A0 positive-only regime; the text-based Chemformer still scores them as strings, so its gains on arms [] are partly string-level artifacts rather than chemical signal.

H4_count_or_distribution_difference.reason: Every treatment arm trains on exactly 394 positives + 394 negatives of one source with identical sampling ratio and budget; candidate-count differences cannot explain between-arm differences. Distribution (difficulty) differences remain and are profiled in difficulty_profile.json.

H5_source_x_scorer_interaction.reason: ANOVA source×scorer interaction p=2.75e-05; best arm is scorer-dependent ({'chemformer': 'A6', 'gnn': 'A1', 'morgan_mlp': 'A5'}).
